scope_statement: The project method is a new repository-owned global spectral/correlation baseline inspired by primary POC literature. It is not a byte-exact or behavior-exact restoration of any historical implementation.

sources.citation: C. D. Kuglin and D. C. Hines, The Phase Correlation Image Alignment Method, Proceedings of the IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Society, 1975, pp. 163-165.
project_use: explicit zero handling when a spectral product is unavailable

sources.citation: K. Takita, T. Aoki, Y. Sasaki, T. Higuchi, and K. Kobayashi, High-Accuracy Subpixel Image Registration Based on Phase-Only Correlation, IEICE Transactions on Fundamentals, E86-A(8), 2003, pp. 1925-1934.
sources.retrieval_status: author-hosted manuscript recovered from a dated web archive and checked against the publisher record
project_use: peak-location refinement as a source-inspired component

sources.citation: K. Ito, A. Morita, T. Aoki, T. Higuchi, H. Nakajima, and K. Kobayashi, A Fingerprint Recognition Algorithm Using Phase-Based Image Matching for Low-Quality Fingerprints, IEICE Transactions on Fundamentals, E87-A(3), 2004, pp. 682-691.
sources.retrieval_status: author-hosted primary paper audited and checked against the publisher record
project_use: peak quality as a similarity signal

source_storage.policy: Audited PDFs and page renders are retained outside the repository; only citations, URLs, hashes, sizes, page counts, and the design audit are versioned.

integrity.canonicalization: UTF-8 JSON, ensure_ascii=true, keys sorted recursively, separators comma and colon; hash the complete document with integrity.canonical_sha256 omitted
